headline: Malware called Gaslight now plants prompt-injection traps to derail the AI tools analysts point at it, while attackers exploit Cisco zero-days and Lantronix industrial converters before defenders can patch.

emerging_trends.text: Attackers have started weaponizing the AI tools defenders lean on, and the macOS implant Gaslight embeds prompt-injection strings that order an analyst's AI assistant to abandon the investigation.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Gaslight Malware Sabotages AI Analysis Tools

emerging_trends.text: Threat actors keep reaching enterprise edge gear before patches land, and CISA's catalog now lists exploited Cisco Unified Communications Manager and PTC Windchill flaws alongside the Catalyst SD-WAN zero-day used for two months in the dark.
emerging_trends.topic_title: CISA Flags Cisco and PTC Flaws as Exploited

emerging_trends.text: Industrial and utility operators face mounting exposure as attackers exploit a Lantronix serial-to-IP converter flaw and CISA warns of root-level holes in EVoke charging stations and Daktronics controllers.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Lantronix Flaw Exploited as ICS Advisories Mount

topics.title: CISA Flags Cisco and PTC Flaws as Exploited
topics.latest_developments: CISA added Cisco Unified Communications Manager server-side request forgery flaw CVE-2026-20230 and PTC Windchill and FlexPLM input-validation flaw CVE-2026-12569 to its known exploited vulnerabilities catalog, as Mandiant detailed how attackers rode Catalyst SD-WAN zero-day CVE-2026-20245 to forge root accounts for two months before disclosure.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-20230 lets unauthenticated attackers forge server-side requests through Cisco Unified Communications Manager, CVE-2026-12569 hits PTC's Windchill and FlexPLM product-lifecycle software, and CVE-2026-20245 grants root on Catalyst SD-WAN via rogue peering. Federal agencies must patch all three under CISA's binding directive.

sources.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited Months Before Patching
Mandiant reveals how Cisco SD-WAN zero-day attacks gained root access
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20245 Exploited to Gain Root Access

topics.title: Lantronix Flaw Exploited as ICS Advisories Mount
topics.latest_developments: Attackers began exploiting CVE-2025-67038 in Lantronix serial-to-IP converters, one of April's BRIDGE:BREAK flaws, the same day CISA published advisories covering EVoke Systems charging-station management software at CVSS 9.4, Daktronics controller firmware granting root access, Schneider Electric PowerLogic P7 relays, and Yokogawa FAST/TOOLS.
topics.summary: CVE-2025-67038 lets attackers reach Lantronix converters that bridge legacy industrial gear to networks. CISA's same-day advisories span EV charging, electronic displays, protection relays, and SCADA software; operators should segment and patch exposed devices.

sources.title: Lantronix Serial-to-IP Converter Flaw Exploited in Attacks After OT Threat Warning
EVoke Systems Charging Station Management System
Daktronics Controller Firmware
Schneider Electric PowerLogic P7

topics.title: Patch Wave Hits Curl, GitLab, and Chrome
topics.latest_developments: Curl shipped fixes for 18 flaws including one that sat in the code for 25 years, GitLab patched 13 bugs across CE and EE with three high-severity defects enabling code execution and information disclosure, and Google's Chrome 149 closed 18 vulnerabilities, more than half of them use-after-free defects.
topics.summary: The 25-year-old curl flaw landed among 18 medium- and low-severity fixes, GitLab's high-severity bugs threaten self-managed instances, and Chrome's use-after-free defects can lead to remote code execution. Update all three now.

sources.title: 25-Year-Old Vulnerability Patched in Curl
GitLab Patches Code Execution, Information Disclosure Vulnerabilities
Chrome 149 Update Resolves 18 Severe Vulnerabilities

topics.title: Gaslight Malware Sabotages AI Analysis Tools
topics.latest_developments: Researchers documented Gaslight, a Rust-based macOS implant and information stealer that hides prompt-injection strings and fake debugging data inside its executable to trick AI-assisted malware analysis tools into aborting or refusing the examination.
topics.summary: Gaslight exfiltrates data while embedding deceptive instructions that tell a malware analyst's AI assistant to refuse the job. Analysts who route samples through AI triage should verify every finding by hand.

sources.title: New macOS malware embeds fake errors to confuse AI analysis tools
New Gaslight macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Disrupt AI-Assisted Analysis

topics.title: Phishing Kits Adopt Browser-in-the-Middle and Trusted Apps
topics.latest_developments: The Bluekit phishing-as-a-service platform added browser-in-the-middle capability and spun up nearly 70 new hostnames in a week to steal logins, while separate actors abused Shopify's Shop order-tracking app, planting fake purchase receipts to lure users into installing remote-access software.
topics.summary: Bluekit rents infrastructure that proxies victims through a real browser session to capture credentials and tokens past multi-factor prompts. Attackers separately seed fake receipts in Shop order histories to start callback-phishing calls; treat unexpected receipts and support numbers as bait.

sources.title: Bluekit phishing kit adopts browser-in-the-middle for login theft
Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks

topics.title: Iranian Handala Targets California Water Utility
topics.latest_developments: California Water Service said Mandiant found no evidence the Iranian hacking group Handala reached operational-technology systems, despite the group's claim that it breached the utility and could disrupt the water supply.
topics.summary: California Water Service, a major U.S. water utility, brought in Mandiant after Handala claimed access and the ability to disrupt supply. The investigation confirmed the intrusion never touched the controls that run treatment and distribution.

sources.title: Cal Water Says No OT Systems Breached in Iranian Handala Cyberattack

weather: Tonight: Scattered Showers And Thunderstorms then Showers And Thunderstorms, low 64F.
Friday: Mostly Cloudy then Isolated Showers And Thunderstorms, high 82F.

games.recap: Ashcraft, Rodriguez, O'Hearn help Pirates rout AL West-leading Mariners 11-1
Brandon Lowe, Henry Davis each homer to lead Pirates over Mariners 5-1

business_politics.title: Iran Strikes Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz
business_politics.latest_developments: Iran hit a container ship with a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz on June 25, and the International Maritime Organization paused the plan to evacuate vessels stranded by the blockade.
business_politics.summary: The strike came hours after Iran warned ships to avoid routes its regime had not sanctioned, testing the deal President Trump brokered to reopen the waterway; Brent crude settled higher even after prices had returned to prewar levels below $72.48 a barrel.

sources.title: Iran Attacks Cargo Ship, Testing Trump's Deal to Reopen Strait
Attack in Strait of Hormuz halts evacuation plan for stranded ships

business_politics.title: Supreme Court Hands Bayer a Roundup Win
business_politics.latest_developments: The U.S. Supreme Court on June 25 overturned a verdict against Bayer over its Roundup weedkiller, a ruling expected to block thousands of cancer-warning lawsuits.
business_politics.summary: The justices threw out the verdict that had anchored billions of dollars in claims alleging Bayer's Monsanto unit failed to warn that Roundup's glyphosate causes cancer, lifting a years-long legal overhang on the German company.

sources.title: Bayer wins crucial US Supreme Court ruling over Roundup weedkiller

business.title: UPMC, Highmark Pledge $45 Million for Public Safety
business.latest_developments: Mayor Corey O'Connor announced June 25 that UPMC and Highmark will give Pittsburgh $45 million over five years to buy fire trucks, ambulances, and other first-responder equipment, on top of $10 million UPMC pledged earlier.
business.summary: The two tax-exempt health giants, long criticized for their sprawling nontaxable property holdings, will fund replacements for the city's aging public safety fleet under the partnership O'Connor unveiled.

sources.title: UPMC, Highmark pledge $45 million to upgrade Pittsburgh's aging fleet of public safety vehicles
O'Connor announces $45M in donations from Highmark, UPMC to buy fire trucks, ambulances

business.title: Hoffmann Family Takes Over the Penguins
business.latest_developments: The Hoffmann family introduced itself June 25 as the Penguins' new principal owners, pledging commitment to Pittsburgh and a continuing role for franchise legend Mario Lemieux.
business.summary: David Hoffmann and his family, completing their purchase from Fenway Sports Group, told fans 'you will win in Pittsburgh' and said they want Lemieux involved for generations to come.

sources.title: As new Penguins owners, the Hoffmann family promises a commitment to winning — and Pittsburgh
New Penguins owners proclaim 'You will win in Pittsburgh'

around_town.title: PennDOT Tests Commercial Street Bridge Slide
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT closed Commercial Street and the Nine Mile Run trail June 25 to test the system that will slide the 22-million-pound replacement span into place, and Pittsburgh Regional Transit released detour guidance for the coming Parkway East closure.
around_town.summary: The Commercial Street Bridge replacement beneath Interstate 376 near Frick Park will shut the Parkway East for nearly a month starting in July; PennDOT's test confirmed the slide mechanism before the move.

sources.title: PennDOT performs major test for upcoming Commercial Street bridge replacement project
Pittsburgh Regional Transit offers advice for travel during Commercial Street Bridge Replacement

around_town.title: Shaler Police Target Route 8 Speeders
around_town.latest_developments: The Shaler Township Police Department launched 'Operation Safe Speed' to crack down on speeding and aggressive driving along Route 8.
around_town.summary: Police Chief Sean Frank said his officers are freshening their patrols on the busy artery that connects Allegheny and Butler counties, aiming to change driving habits on one of the area's heaviest-traveled roads.

sources.title: Police cracking down on speeding, aggressive driving on busy Route 8

events.title: Frzy Opens Free Concert at the Point
events.latest_developments: Pittsburgh rapper Frzy joined the bill as an opener for the free concert at Point State Park this weekend, the Post-Gazette's things-to-do guide reported.
events.summary: The performance is part of the free, ticketed Commonwealth Concert Series at Point State Park downtown, a weekend lineup that also marks the Andy Warhol Bridge's 100th birthday across the city, June 25-28.

sources.title: Pittsburgh-area things to do this weekend: Warhol Bridge turns 100, Frzy opens concert at the Point, more

events.title: 'Open Columns' Completes Airport Art Program
events.latest_developments: Artist Patrick Marold's 'Open Columns' installation completed the public-art transformation at Pittsburgh International Airport's new terminal.
events.summary: The work caps the art program built into the recently opened terminal, and travelers can view it as they move through the building in Findlay Township.

sources.title: 'Open Columns' completes Pittsburgh International Airport's art transformation

around_teams.title: Eric Ebron Joins Not Just Football
around_teams.latest_developments: Former tight end Eric Ebron sat down with Cam Heyward's 'Not Just Football' podcast for a wide-ranging talk on his career and his retirement at 28.
around_teams.summary: Ebron shared locker-room stories from Detroit, Indianapolis, and Pittsburgh, reflected on Andrew Luck's retirement, and touched on Bill Belichick taking over at North Carolina.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Eric Ebron Talks Andrew Luck's Retirement, The 11-0 Steelers Team, & More! | Not Just Football

around_teams.title: Konnor Griffin Hits in Altoona Rehab
around_teams.latest_developments: Pirates prospect Konnor Griffin excelled at the plate in a rehab start at Double-A Altoona, the Post-Gazette reported.
around_teams.summary: Griffin's progress offers a bright spot for an injury-riddled Pirates roster that placed first baseman Spencer Horwitz on the injured list this week.

sources.title: Konnor Griffin excels at the plate in rehab start at Double-A Altoona

team_usa.title: USMNT Closes Group D Against Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: The U.S. men's national team played its Group D finale against Türkiye on June 25, with manager Mauricio Pochettino rotating his lineup and resting four yellow-carded players to keep them eligible for the round of 32.
team_usa.summary: Having already clinched a knockout spot as a World Cup co-host, the United States used the match to manage suspensions ahead of its round-of-32 game July 1, with ESPN noting Türkiye presents a test similar to the tournament's best sides.

sources.title: What Türkiye match will reveal about USMNT's knockout round hopes
Pochettino won't 'risk' USMNT players on yellows

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Figma chief executive Dylan Field discusses building the design company and makes the case that AI is a tailwind for Figma rather than a threat to it.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues that much of the tech and AI industry imitates the surface rituals and spending of past successes without the underlying substance or returns to justify them.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport urges AI companies to stop publicizing alarming claims about their own products' dangers, likening it to a carmaker warning that its bestselling truck might be catastrophically unsafe.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
